title: Traveling with Kyle - Living in Chile part 2/17

transcript.text: Yeah, I bumped into my Chilean buddy, who I was hanging out with in Bukong, now he's selling his bamboo saxophones, which sounded just like saxophones. I don't know how he figured this out. Um, I should have bought one, but I didn't have the money at the time. But, um, and he was selling digi-deos out of bamboo that they grew out there, and flutes and all this stuff just on the street.

And so I started, I hung out with him a couple of times at my apartment, and we went out one day. And, uh, oh, at this point I had a job teaching at the IRS building. But, um, he calls me up one day. I guess he's like really, he, yeah, he calls me up one day. He's like, oh, well, well, before when I first met this kid who I was living with, he said that, uh,

He had a friend, a female friend from England who was, like, traveling around the world for five years, and she got around by selling, um, jewelry that she made. And I've met other people like that, like my Colombian friends, who I bought a big ol' bag of weed off of La Paz, were doing that.
And I saw those guys again in, actually, in Rio, and was hanging out with them for a couple of nights. That was cool. Uh, small world.
But, um, and then I actually met a German girl who was doing that. Uh, just getting by by selling, like, jewelry that she made. Um, and she said she was in some, um, Paraguay.
And there are these certain, uh, I guess, Mennonites, like the ones who live in, um, Belize. And then, but there's Mennonite ones in, uh, Paraguay who are white people.
And they've been there a couple hundred years. And, but they originally come from Germany. And she was talking to them and couldn't understand a single thing they were saying.
They said by then their German language was totally different. But anyways, um, yeah, he said he had this friend who was doing that.
Who would, like, um, travel around. I guess she was heading towards Santiago. And she was thinking about teaching English there.
And, like, if it was okay, if she would stay with us, then we would help her get started or something.
And anyways, um, my friend, who I met in Pukong, calls me up one night. And he goes, oh, yeah, I met this girl.
She wants to teach English. And I know you teach English. Why don't you come and meet her? Maybe you can, you know, help her out.
And so I go down there and I see this girl. And, uh, and she's like, yeah, um, do you know this kid from Montana? So-and-so.
And I'm like, oh, no. Yeah, that's my roommate. It's my world. So, we, we, we take the subway up there.
And he comes back. And, uh, yeah, it's his friend. And, um, oh, but before that, actually, that's, like, right after I met my girlfriend.
Um, yeah, because this kid, I guess when he first came down to Santiago, and we were still living at the hotel, we went out to the disco one night.
And, uh, what was it called? There's two disco areas. There's Bella Vista, and then there's, which we went to a lot in the beginning.
But then there's, um, then there's the other one, which I can't remember, which is higher up.

And, uh, we went out there, and I was really drunk, but I do notice, like, there's this girl, like, hitting on my friend, and then they, anyway, she comes back to a hotel with him, and her friend does, too.
And I was really drunk. I thought she was going to go back with me, but she ended up buying me a hamburger or a hot dog, and then she went home, like, she only lived, like, four blocks down.
But anyways, uh, yeah, and there were these, these children were, like, really jealous. Like, some kid ran up and kicked my foot out from under me, and I was like, what are you doing?
And his friend was like, I don't want my damn, he's just drunk.
But, um, anyways, uh, yeah, she, uh, she, uh, is his girlfriend, and so she's hanging out with him, I think, for, like, two months.
How long are we living together? No, we're living together for, like, one or two months or something. A long time. Like, two months.

And she, she's bringing these Chilean girls over. She was, like, dead set on finding me a girlfriend. So she brings one girl over, but she was just totally, like, into cocaine, just talked too much. Just crazy.
So then she brings this other girl, and she was just talking to her friend. And then we go out for some drinks with her other friend, who had a Norwegian boyfriend.
And then, like, a week later, she's like, oh, yeah, she's going to bring that girl over. I'm like, that girl? With the Norwegian guy? Oh, wow, she's hot. So I was like, cool.
So then she comes over, she brings her over, and, uh, I'm, uh, and, uh, she was actually kind of, like, you know, being, she was grounded.
Yeah, she was, like, an actual person, not, like, just talking to her friend, you know, she was, like, into me.
So anyways, uh, she invites herself to stay over there, and she's, and so she's my girlfriend.
Anyways, uh, yeah, but I was only with her for, like, a week when this English girl moves in.
And, uh, she's there for, like, three weeks or something, and, uh, I get her a job at an institute, which I didn't take the job there, because I just didn't pay enough.
But it was good for her, because she didn't have a bike at the time, and it was only, like, a block down.
And, uh, and she got a bunch of private classes.

It's really easy to get a new job in teaching English if you're a girl, because most of the people who take classes are, you know, business people who are men, and so they always want to talk to the women.
So, uh, she gets her jobs teaching English, and she's living with us.
Um, I had my own room, though, because the first two months he had, it was only a one-room apartment on the corner.
It was one room, a bathroom, a deck outside, and a really small living room connected to a really small kitchen that didn't even have a stove or anything.
Like, I had to cook with my, with my, uh, my camp stove.

And, um, so it's a tiny little place, but I was having my own room there, because he had, it was like, we were going to be there four months, and he was going to have the bedroom for the first two months, and I was going to have the bedroom for the second two months.
So, I had moved in the bedroom, so they're living in the living room.
But, uh, she's staying with us for free, because I guess, you know, she doesn't have any money.
Um, and, uh, but I tried to get them weed one night, and I asked them if they had, and they didn't, so I went out to get some.
And I came back, and it turned out that they were smoking some.
So, and I was like, okay, so I just went and laid in bed, and I closed the door.
But then they opened it, and I'm cold, and they kept closing it.
So, anyways, I got angry with them.
It's kind of a mistake, because after that, after that, they were kind of, like, really angry with me, and not hanging out with me.
And then he, he invites this English guy over.
So then there's, like, four of us living in the same apartment.
And he's there two weeks.
So then I'm like, okay, I'm going to invite my friend over.
So, my friend starts living with us.
So now there's five people living in a one-bedroom apartment.

And, uh, so, and, um, and, you know, by now, my roommate's really pissed off at me, because I had, because there were so many people living there, even though they're all, most of them were his friends.
And, um, then he starts to kind of go nuts, because he's, like, I guess he's on cocaine, and only getting, like, four hours of sleep a night.
And he'd come back at, like, 10 o'clock at night, every single night, and then get up at 6 o'clock the next morning.
I guess he was able to get off on that much sleep, because he was on cocaine the whole time.
But, uh, he's kind of starting to go nuts.
And one night, his girlfriend, he invites his girlfriend over, who is friends with my friend, my girlfriend.
Oh, but before that, this funny thing happened.
I actually explained it in another video, but the, actually, I explained it in another video, so I won't explain it again.
But, uh, yeah, he brings his girlfriend over.
And, uh, no, he invites his girlfriend over.
So his girlfriend comes over, and my girlfriend's there, and my friend's there.
So there's the four of us.
And he never shows up.
And, uh, so he calls me, and I'm like, oh, yeah, here she is.
And then he talks to her for, like, a second.
And then I have the phone.
He's like, hey, asshole, what the fuck are you doing?
Giving the phone to her.
I called you.
I was like, what?
He's like, yeah, how long are you going to be there?
I'm like, dude, I live here.
I'm going to be here all night.
He's like, ah, all right, bye.
Anyways, my, I guess by then, his girlfriend had gotten pissed off at him for, like, not ever showing up.
So she just.
